messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agents window with Remote SSH fails after session materialization: Copilot CLI session is not authenticated ISSUE BODY: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No, requires Remote SSH, GitHub Copilot Chat, etc - VS Code Version: 1.122.0 - OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 (and the remote is Amazon Linux 2023) ## Summary When I use the Agents window with a Remote SSH host, the SSH connection and remote Agent Host appear to start successfully, and a provisional Copilot CLI session is created and materialized. However, the first message fails with a Copilot authentication error from the Agents Host output: ``` Session error: authentication - Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider ``` Regular GitHub Copilot Chat works in VS Code, and Copilot CLI also works from the integrated terminal on the same remote host. The failure appears specific to the Agents window + Remote SSH + Copilot CLI Agent Host path. ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open the Agents window. 2. Select a Remote SSH host from the SSH config. 3. Select a folder on the remote host. 4. Start a new Copilot CLI session. 5. Send an arbitrary message, for example `Hello?`. ### Expected Result The message should be sent to the remote Copilot CLI session, and the Agents window should show the assistant response. ### Actual Result The session fails immediately after materialization. In VS Code 1.121.0, the authentication error was shown as a response message in the Agents window. In VS Code 1.122.0, the error is no longer shown in the session UI; instead, the session panel returns to the new-session panel immediately. The underlying error can be seen in the Agents Host output channel. The screenshot shows that the Agents window is connected to a Remote SSH workspace, Copilot CLI is selected, the remote file tree is visible, and the Agent Host terminal starts successfully on the remote host. ## Relevant Agents Host Output I redacted the remote host name and repository path. ``` [2026-05-28T05:01:02.195Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Listing sessions... [2026-05-28T05:01:02.196Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Auth token updated [2026-05-28T05:01:02.196Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Listing models... [2026-05-28T05:01:02.196Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Starting CopilotClient... (with token) [2026-05-28T05:01:02.200Z] [ERROR] [ProtocolServer] Request 'listSessions' failed Authentication is required to use Copilot [2026-05-28T05:01:02.200Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Resolved CLI path: /home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/@github/copilot/index.js [2026-05-28T05:01:03.323Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] CopilotClient started successfully [2026-05-28T05:01:03.514Z] [ERROR] Error: Request models.list failed with message: Not authenticated. Please authenticate first. at handleResponse (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:565:48) at handleMessage (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:345:13) at processMessageQueue (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:362:17) at Immediate. (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:334:13) at process.processImmediate (node:internal/timers:484:21) [Copilot] Failed to refresh models [2026-05-28T05:01:44.357Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Creating session... [2026-05-28T05:01:44.368Z] [ERROR] [ProtocolServer] Request 'subscribe' failed Session not found on backend: copilotcli:/5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047 (...omitted unrelated resourceRead/resourceList errors for missing optional settings files...) [2026-05-28T05:01:44.376Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Session created (provisional): copilotcli:/5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047 (...omitted unrelated resourceRead/resourceList errors for missing optional settings files...) [2026-05-28T05:01:44.756Z] [INFO ] [TerminalManager] Creating terminal agenthost-terminal:/61fb4a95-541f-435a-b496-a1ce41afc67b: shell=/bin/bash, cwd=/home//, cols=80, rows=30 [2026-05-28T05:01:44.757Z] [INFO ] [TerminalManager] Shell integration injected for agenthost-terminal:/61fb4a95-541f-435a-b496-a1ce41afc67b (...omitted unrelated resourceRead/resourceList errors for missing optional settings files...) [2026-05-28T05:01:53.990Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Listing sessions... [2026-05-28T05:01:54.003Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Found 0 sessions [2026-05-28T05:01:54.140Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] setClientTools: clientId=7b3f01f5-4187-4260-ae32-fcc238618cec, tools=[problems, runTask, getTaskOutput, openBrowserPage], hasCachedSdkSession=false [2026-05-28T05:01:54.165Z] [INFO ] [AgentPluginManager] Syncing plugin: vscode-synced-customization:/remote-ssh__-copilotcli → file:///home//.vscode-server/data/agentPlugins/vscode-synced-customization-remote-ssh--copilotcli [2026-05-28T05:01:54.265Z] [ERROR] [AgentPluginManager] Failed to sync plugin vscode-synced-customization:/remote-ssh__-copilotcli: Access to vscode-synced-customization:/ is not granted. [2026-05-28T05:01:54.965Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Session materialized: copilotcli:/5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047 [2026-05-28T05:01:54.969Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] sendMessage: cachedEntry=true, hasActiveClient=true, activeClientId=(set) [2026-05-28T05:01:54.970Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] sendMessage called: "Hello?" (0 attachments) [2026-05-28T05:01:54.982Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] rpc.mode.set succeeded: mode=interactive [2026-05-28T05:01:54.991Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] session.send() returned [2026-05-28T05:01:54.991Z] [ERROR] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] Session error: authentication - Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider ``` ## Additional Notes - GitHub Copilot Chat works normally in the regular VS Code window. - Copilot CLI also works when I run it manually in the integrated terminal on the same remote host. - The remote Agent Host terminal is created successfully, so the Remote SSH connection itself appears to be working. - The log suggests that VS Code receives or updates a Copilot auth token, starts `@github/copilot/index.js` with token-related context, and materializes the provisional session, but the Copilot CLI/SDK backend still treats the session as unauthenticated. - This looks different from #316223, where selecting the SSH host did not proceed. In this case, the connection proceeds further and fails after session materialization. - It may be related to #317297 because both involve Agents window + Remote SSH + Copilot CLI routing, but in this case the request appears to reach the remote Agent Host and fails as an unauthenticated Copilot session rather than being silently dropped before reaching the remote CLI. --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Launching VS Code Extensions for Debugging To debug a VS Code extension via npx @playwright/cli, launch VS Code Insiders with `--extensionDevelopmentPath` pointing to your extension source and `--remote-debugging-port` for CDP. Use `--user-data-dir` to avoid conflicting with an already-running VS Code instance. ```bash # Build the extension first (from the repo root) npm run compile # Launch VS Code Insiders with the extension and CDP # IMPORTANT: Use a persistent directory (not /tmp) so auth state is preserved. # .vscode-ext-debug is relative to the project root — works in worktrees and is gitignored. code-insiders \ --extensionDevelopmentPath="$PWD" \ --remote-debugging-port=9223 \ --user-data-dir="$PWD/.vscode-ext-debug" # Wait for VS Code to start, retry until attached for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do npx @playwright/cli attach --cdp=http://127.0.0.1:9223 2>/dev/null && break || sleep 3; done # Verify you're connected to the right target (not about:blank) # If `tab-list` shows the wrong target, run `npx @playwright/cli close` and reattach npx @playwright/cli tab-list npx @playwright/cli snapshot ``` **Key flags:** - `--extensionDevelopmentPath=` — loads your extension from source (must be compiled first). Use `$PWD` when running from the repo root. - `--remote-debugging-port=9223` — enables CDP (use 9223 to avoid conflicts with other apps on 9222) - `--user-data-dir=` — uses a separate profile so it starts a new process instead of sending to an existing VS Code instance. **Always use a persistent path** (e.g., `$PWD/.vscode-ext-debug`) rather than `/tmp/...` so authentication, settings, and extension state survive across sessions. **Without `--user-data-dir`**, VS Code detects the running instance, forwards the args to it, and exits immediately — you'll see "Sent env to running instance. Terminating..." and CDP never starts. > **⚠️ Authentication is required.** The Copilot Chat extension needs an authenticated GitHub session to function. Using a temp directory (e.g., `/tmp/...`) creates a fresh profile with no auth — the agent will hit a "Sign in to use Copilot" wall and model resolution will fail with "Language model unavailable." > > **Always use a persistent `--user-data-dir`** like `$PWD/.vscode-ext-debug`. On first use, launch once and sign in manually. Subsequent launches will reuse the auth session. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Copilot CLI (Background Agent) The Copilot CLI SDK runs in the same VS Code process and produces a rich trace hierarchy including subagents, permissions, hooks, and tool calls: ``` copilot-chat invoke_agent copilotcli [~45s] ← extension wrapper └── github-copilot invoke_agent [~42s] ← SDK native spans ├── chat claude-sonnet-4.6 [~16s] │ ├── hook postToolUse ← hook execution │ └── hook postToolUse ├── execute_tool task [~18s] │ └── invoke_agent task ← subagent │ ├── chat claude-sonnet-4.6 │ ├── execute_tool bash │ │ └── permission │ └── execute_tool report_intent ├── chat claude-sonnet-4.6 [~4s] └── hook sessionEnd ← session lifecycle hook ``` The extension wrapper span (`invoke_agent copilotcli`, service `copilot-chat`) parents the SDK's native spans (service `github-copilot`). Both appear in the same trace in your collector. **Agent Debug Log panel**: CLI sessions show the full SDK hierarchy in the Tree View — identical to what appears in Grafana/Jaeger. This works even when OTel export is disabled, because the SDK's internal tracing is always active for the debug panel. > **Content in the debug panel**: When OTel export is disabled (the default), the debug panel automatically captures full prompt/response content. When OTel export is enabled, content capture is controlled by the `captureContent` setting — the same flag applies to both the debug panel and OTLP export. To see content in the debug panel while OTel is enabled, set `github.copilot.chat.otel.captureContent` to `true`. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Copilot CLI Integration This folder contains the Copilot CLI integration for VS Code Chat. It enables users to open a new Chat window and interact with a Copilot CLI agent instance directly within VS Code. **VS Code provides the UI, Copilot CLI SDK provides the smarts.** > **Important:** The Copilot CLI agent functionality is powered by the `@github/copilot/sdk` package. See the SDK package for full type definitions. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

response:
